select_when: The same message, prompt, or procedure is landing very differently across experience levels and the support level may be mismatched to expertise.
Performance is dropping because experts are being slowed by redundant explanation or because novices are being under-supported.
A training, workflow, or prompt should deliberately remove supports that have become drag while retaining structure for the less experienced user.
Context volume is rising, but clarity depends more on fit to expertise than on adding still more examples or scaffolding.
avoid_when: Less guidance is being treated as automatically better even though the task, stakes, or novelty still require renewed structure.
An expert-facing simplification is being generalized to novices who still need concrete steps, examples, or explicit distinctions.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on expertise-sensitive support design, redundant-load removal, and fit between mastery level and explanation depth.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor prompt-engineering sprawl because the raw source already supports the core mismatch logic without widening into general AI-advice territory.
open_questions: The raw source is especially strong on prompt and instruction design, but the Wave 1 file keeps that general enough to stay reusable across human and machine workflows.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that the model is strongest when the same message lands differently across expertise levels and when support has become drag.

avoid_when.note: The danger line about confusing less guidance with better guidance is explicit, as is the novice-versus-expert mismatch.

input_type.note: The source is explicit about expertise mismatches across prompts, training, and procedures; the typed input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The output is a retuned level of guidance and compression calibrated to mastery; the short label compresses that operational move.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses fit problems inside larger support systems, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
